column2: An in vitro drug release study was carried out over 24 h using phosphate buffered saline (PBS) as
the release medium. The contact lenses were immersed in 10 mL of PBS and placed in a mechanical
shaking bath. The release of prednisolone from the NP alone was also detected by placing 0.4 g of
NP in a dialysis bag and placing this bag in a plastic tube containing 10 mL of PBS. This tube was
also placed together with the contact lenses in the shaker which was set at 80 cycles/min at 35.5 ˝C.

column1: Aliquots of 0.5 mL were taken at set time-intervals (0.5, 1, 1.5, 3, 6 and 24 h) and replaced by equivalent

column2: volumes of fresh PBS. Withdrawn samples were diluted with 2 mL of methanol, then run through
HPLC to quantify concentration of prednisolone released.
